div.h2: Three-Level View: One read() Syscall Stop

ul.li: T₁ = clock_gettime at entry stop (after PTRACE_GETREGS returns to tracer)
T₂ = clock_gettime at exit stop (after PTRACE_GETREGS returns to tracer)

li.strong: T₂ − T₁ includes 2× context-switch round-trips (~2–20 μs each)

ul.li: For read() blocking 500 ms on disk: T₂−T₁ ≈ 500 ms (I/O dominates ✓)
For getpid() taking 100 ns: T₂−T₁ ≈ 4–20 μs (overhead dominates ✗) Ratio is meaningful. Absolute nanoseconds are not.

div.p: ~1–10 μs / context switch 2 switches per syscall stop = 2–20 μs observer overhead
